inline_comments.message: I admire that you create a separate disk just for that but wouldn't using /tmp be just enough?;)

CI minions do not have /tmp configured to be tmpfs, it can be verified in any job log, e.g. https://jenkins.onap.org/view/oom/job/oom-master-commit-lint/120/consoleText:


---> df -h:
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
udev            2.0G     0  2.0G   0% /dev
tmpfs           395M  652K  394M   1% /run
/dev/vda1        20G  7.6G   12G  40% /
tmpfs           2.0G     0  2.0G   0% /dev/shm
tmpfs           5.0M     0  5.0M   0% /run/lock
tmpfs           2.0G     0  2.0G   0% /sys/fs/cgroup
/dev/vda15      105M  3.6M  101M   4% /boot/efi
tmpfs           395M     0  395M   0% /run/user/1001

Hmm interesting I thought that all cloud images mount tmpfs in /tmp. Thanks for clarification!

So my only suggestion would be to increase the storage size to at least 128 just to be sure that we don't hit the limit after some short time period
I obviously knew that when I proposed the change (cough cough) ;-)
